title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 win over Minnesota United
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated Minnesota United 2-1 at Gillette Stadium with goals from Carles Gil and Luca Langoni.
Revolution's aggressive counterattack play led to Langoni's decisive goal after exploiting a midfield turnover by Minnesota.
Goalkeeper Matt Turner delivered crucial saves, helping secure the win despite criticism from a previous match miscue.

title: SNL recap: Best moments from Will Ferrell hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’ season finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Will Ferrell hosted the 'SNL' Season 51 finale on May 16, with Paul McCartney as musical guest.
Ferrell's cold open featured him as the ghost of Jeffrey Epstein with James Austin Johnson as Donald Trump.
Paul McCartney performed three songs, including 'Band on the Run' and a track from his upcoming album.

title: Flashback: A look at previous times Tall Ships have sailed into Boston
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 will occur July 11-16, featuring the Sail250 fleet from over 25 countries for the U.S. 250th anniversary.
Previous Sail Boston events took place in 1976, 1980, 1992, 2000, 2009, 2012, and 2017, with varying public and political reactions.
The 1980 event attracted about 2 million spectators, exceeding attendance numbers from the inaugural 1976 celebration.

title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returns to Patriots rookie minicamp, improving team morale after recent off-field issues.
The Patriots are reportedly negotiating a trade for wide receiver A.J. Brown to bolster their roster.
Newly acquired safety Kevin Byard praises Vrabel's energetic coaching style, unchanged from their Tennessee days.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive 426-foot home run in the eighth inning, leading the Red Sox to a 3-2 victory.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two earned runs and striking out three against the Braves.
Aroldis Chapman secured the save after a tense bases-loaded situation in the ninth inning, preserving the Red Sox win.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story placed on 10-day IL with sports hernia, absence may lead to surgery decision.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story for the Red Sox.
Story's batting average is .206 with a .244 on-base percentage, struggling defensively with six errors.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died in a head-on collision between a pickup truck and a school bus in Marlborough, NH.
The 13 students on the bus from Monadnock Regional Middle High School were uninjured, police confirmed.
The crash occurred on Route 12; authorities are investigating with no serious student injuries reported.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Braves after Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning.
Braves' Mike Yastrzemski, Carl Yastrzemski's grandson, secured his seventh career walk-off hit.
Boston's Connelly Early pitched five innings, giving up two home runs and striking out six batters.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in rejoining the Patriots, but the A.J. Brown trade complicates the possibility.
The Patriots' potential trade for A.J. Brown and his $32 million contract limits cap space for Diggs.
Diggs, coming off a 1,000-yard season, may not settle for a role as the third receiver in New England.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave, Dorchester, and is reported missing.
He is described as Black, 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair, known to visit several local areas.
Boston Police urge anyone with information to contact District B-3 detectives or use CrimeStoppers for anonymous tips.
